stemHtml: Your aircraft is in a climb to FL90 with the autopilot engaged. As you climb through FL75 ATC asks you to stop climb at FL80. What is the most appropriate action to take?

options.a: Disconnect the autopilot and manually level off the aircraft at 8000 ft to prevent overshoot.
options.b: Ignore the new clearance and continue to FL090, since it was previously cleared.
options.c: Maintain current vertical speed and select FL080 once passing FL079 to minimize workload.
options.d: Promptly adjust the autopilot target altitude to FL080 and monitor vertical speed to avoid level bust.
